title: CurvatureIntegral
summary: Specializes the finite-part framework to renormalized integrals of curvature invariants.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes the finite-part framework to renormalized integrals of curvature invariants.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/CurvatureIntegral.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Cutoff-integral data for a chosen invariant on a PE normal form. Its mathematical data are: normal Form: Poincare Normal Form Data. u, v, w X; cutoff Integral: Pos Real maps to Real; cutoff Expansion: Laurent Log Expansion Real.
correspondence.leanType: structure CurvatureIntegralCutoffData (X : PESpace.{u, v}) {Invariant : Type z} (I : Invariant)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Curvature integration as a generic cutoff functional. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace. u, v, invariant a type, and i of type invariant, its defining expression is cutoff Value : equals the function sending D to D.cutoff Integral expansion : equals the function sending D to D.cutoff Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: def curvatureIntegralCutoffFunctional (X : PESpace.{u, v}) {Invariant : Type z} (I : Invariant) : CutoffFunctional (CurvatureIntegralCutoffData.{u, v, w, z} X I) Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The renormalized integral of a chosen curvature invariant. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, invariant a type, i of type invariant, and d of type curvature integral cutoff data x i, its defining expression is curvature Integral Cutoff Functional X I.renormalized Value D.
correspondence.leanType: def RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The logarithmic anomaly of a curvature integral. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, invariant a type, i of type invariant, and d of type curvature integral cutoff data x i, its defining expression is curvature Integral Cutoff Functional X I.anomaly Value D.
correspondence.leanType: def CurvatureIntegralAnomaly {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For X of type PESpace, Invariant a type, I of type Invariant, and D of type Curvature Integral Cutoff Data X I, Renormalized Curvature Integral D equals finite Part D.cutoff Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral_eq_finitePart {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral D = finitePart D.cutoffExpansion

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For X of type PESpace, Invariant a type, I of type Invariant, and D of type Curvature Integral Cutoff Data X I, Curvature Integral Anomaly D equals log Anomaly D.cutoff Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CurvatureIntegralAnomaly_eq_logAnomaly {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : CurvatureIntegralAnomaly D = logAnomaly D.cutoffExpansion

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
